insights.recommendation: Reallocate budget from LinkedIn to Referral and Recruiter Outreach channels to improve overall hiring performance.

evidence.key_insight: Referral has the highest offer rate at 41.2% and the lowest dropout, while LinkedIn has the lowest offer rate at 12.5%, indicating it's not a viable channel for investment.

alternative.trade_off: Higher cost for potentially more detailed insights but at a significant increase in budget.

evaluation.reasoning: The recommendation to reallocate budget from LinkedIn aligns well with the evidence of its low offer rate. It matches the expected themes and entities from the golden dataset.

insights.recommendation: Revamp headcount planning and communication processes to address role cancellations and compensation mismatches, which account for 26.3% of your rejections.

evidence.key_insight: Critically high process failures such as role cancellations and mismatches, which meet or exceed typical industry thresholds for controllable rejections, suggest internal process issues.

alternative.trade_off: Less contextual understanding and might miss industry-specific nuances, potentially leading to less targeted recommendations.

insights.rag_context: [{"type":"text","text":"{\"pageContent\":\"Rejection root cause categorisation. Controllable rejections (team can fix): Role cancelled (15) — headcount planning failure; Compensation mismatch (15) — salary band not communicated early; Communication issues (12) — interviewer training gap; No show (9) — scheduling/engagement failure. Total controllable: 51 rejections (44.7% of all rejections). Candidate-driven rejections: Underqualified (13), Technical skills (11), Overqualified (10), Culture fit (10), Better candidate selected (8). Total: 52 (45.6%). Recommendation: half of all rejections are internally fixable.\",\"metadata\":{\"agent\":\"rejection_pattern\",\"category\":\"controllable_vs_uncontrollable\"},\"id\":\"rej-003\"}"},{"type":"text","text":"{\"pageContent\":\"Rejection reason breakdown (114 rejections from 200 candidates, 57% rejection rate). Top reasons: Role cancelled (15, 13.2%), Compensation mismatch (15, 13.2%), Underqualified (13, 11.4%), Communication issues (12, 10.5%), Technical skills (11, 9.6%), Background check failed (11, 9.6%), Culture fit (10, 8.8%), Overqualified (10, 8.8%), No show (9, 7.9%), Better candidate selected (8, 7.0%). Role cancellations and compensation mismatches together account for 26.3% — these are process failures, not candidate failures.\",\"metadata\":{\"agent\":\"rejection_pattern\",\"category\":\"rejection_reasons_overall\"},\"id\":\"rej-001\"}"},{"type":"text","text":"{\"pageContent\":\"Industry benchmarks for rejection patterns in technical hiring. Typical rejection rates: Engineering roles 55-65%, Data Science 50-60%, Product 45-55%. Expected early-stage (R1) rejection rate: 35-45% — screens out unqualified applicants. Expected compensation mismatch rate: 5-10% — above 12% signals JD salary range not being shared. Background check failure rate benchmark: 2-4%; above 8% suggests insufficient pre-screening. Culture fit rejection rate: 5-10%; above 12% may signal inconsistent culture definition across panels. No-show benchmark: under 5%; above 7% suggests poor candidate engagement or scheduling friction.\",\"metadata\":{\"agent\":\"rejection_pattern\",\"category\":\"industry_rejection_benchmarks\"},\"id\":\"rej-004\"}"},{"type":"text","text":"{\"pageContent\":\"Recommended actions to reduce avoidable rejections. (1) Add salary range to JD upfront — eliminates most compensation mismatch rejections. (2) Improve Round 1 screening criteria — 20% rejection at R1 suggests JD clarity issue. (3) Implement interviewer calibration sessions — communication and culture fit vary by panel. (4) Add a 24-hour reminder for scheduled interviews — reduces no-shows. (5) Review role cancellation process — 15 cancelled roles wastes candidate pipeline and brand trust. (6) Add an overqualified/underqualified pre-screen question at application stage.\",\"metadata\":{\"agent\":\"rejection_pattern\",\"category\":\"rejection_improvement_actions\"},\"id\":\"rej-005\"}"}]

evaluation.reasoning: The insight correctly identifies process failures as a significant issue and aligns with the golden dataset expectations, including the mention of required entities.
